title: [CI:DOCS] Rewrite --uidmap doc in podman-create.1.md and podman-run.1.md
body:
I've tried to improve the documentation of the **--uidmap** option in podman-create.1.md and podman-run.1.md.


* Introduce the concept of "intermediate UID" to explain how --uidmap works when running rootless.

* Add Markdown tables to show examples of how UIDs are mapped.

(I don't know if there is a better word than "intermediate UID". I'm not a native English speaker)


Signed-off-by: Erik Sjölund <erik.sjolund@gmail.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
LGTM
and happy green test buttons.

I just noticed that the order of the columns in the tables are different.
I should fix that.
The column "container UID" should be to the right.

@TomSweeneyRedHat  What do you think? Should  _from_uid_ be replaced inside the tables?

For example from this:
|   host UID     |    container UID    |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| -              | -                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| _from_uid_     | _container_uid_     |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| _from_uid_ + 1 | _container_uid_ + 1 |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| _from_uid_ + 2 | _container_uid_ + 2 |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| _from_uid_ + 3 | _container_uid_ + 3 |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
to this:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
|   host UID     |    container UID    |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| -              | -                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| _host_uid_     | _container_uid_     |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| _host_uid_ + 1 | _container_uid_ + 1 |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| _host_uid_ + 2 | _container_uid_ + 2 |                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| _host_uid_ + 3 | _container_uid_ + 3 |            


I don't have any strong preference.
@eriksjolund as the `from_uid` can be one of two values dependent upon the type of user, I'd stick with from_uid there.  My $.02, YMMV.
I came to the same conclusion after thinking a bit more. I've now no more edits to make.
